content:
- Marked task-05 completed with all acceptance boxes checked and `<passes>true</passes>`.
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which refreshed the Ralph task summaries and selected the next task in `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next: stage all modified files including Ralph metadata and the plan, commit with test evidence and the verify-image harness challenge noted, then push.

- Completed task-05 end-to-end and pushed commit `4addba1` to `master`.
- Final state: verify image ENTRYPOINT now exposes `verify-service`, README/compose teach explicit `validate-config` and `run`, Rust operator/image contracts match that surface, the Go CLI has a regression test for rejecting bare root flags, and the verify-image runtime harness uses `run --config ...`.
- Verification evidence already recorded in the commit message and progress log: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed; no `make test-long` lane was run.

- Read prior codex progress, `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, the four active story-03 docs task files, and the current README/operator-surface sections.
- Confirmed selection precedence: there are zero unblocked failing bug tasks, four unblocked failing non-bug tasks, and no visible `<blocked_by>` or `<priority>` tags on those four task files.
- Determined the actual repo split is a Rust runner workspace plus a Go `cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice` API. Based on the current README surface, the highest-pressure missing contract is runner webhook payload documentation because `/ingest/<mapping_id>` is exposed without a payload spec. Next action if resuming after context loss: continue from story-03 docs by executing task-07 first, then likely task-08/09 before task-10 because TLS reference wants cross-links to payload and OpenAPI docs.

- Read prior codex progress and confirmed the newly selected task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Loaded improve-code-boundaries skill instructions; task-07 is the active work item under story-03 docs/API contracts.
- Next: read task-07 file, determine whether it is TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE, then follow the required branch immediately.

- Read task-07 and confirmed it is not in NOW EXECUTE state; the task currently has no plan path attached.
- Inspected README Runner Quick Start to locate the missing webhook payload section and confirm the public surface currently only lists GET /healthz and POST /ingest/<mapping_id>.
- Next: inspect an existing task plus plan pair, then create task-07 plan file, attach it to the task, and stop in TO BE VERIFIED state per Ralph instructions.

- Inspected runner webhook payload parser, routing/runtime response mapping, and README operator-surface contract helpers.
- Confirmed the payload surface is row-batch vs resolved, `op` accepts c/u/r/d, same-table batches are enforced by routing, `length` must match payload length, and ingest error bodies currently return plain text strings rather than JSON.
- README word budget is tight at 1243 words against a 1250-word contract, so the execution plan must include removing/merging muddy prose while adding the webhook payload section.

- Created the task-07 plan file at .ralph/tasks/story-03-docs-api-contracts/task-07-docs-webhook-payload-format_plans/2026-04-25-webhook-payload-format-plan.md and attached it to the task file.
- Marked the plan as NOW EXECUTE because the interface is stable: use a `### Webhook Payload Format` subsection under Runner Quick Start, keep the existing 3 second-level headings, and stay under the 1250-word README budget by compressing nearby prose.
- Critical execution notes for the next turn: follow the plan with vertical TDD slices only, keep docs aligned with the current plain-text ingest error bodies, do not invent JSON error responses, and stop immediately if the README word-budget/shape constraint proves incompatible with accurate payload docs.

- Read prior codex progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active work is `task-07-docs-webhook-payload-format` and prior context says its plan was promoted to NOW EXECUTE; reading the task file and plan now.
- Next: execute the plan with vertical TDD slices, keep the README within the word-budget/heading constraints, and stop only after checks pass or the design must revert to TO BE VERIFIED.

- Read the active task file, attached NOW EXECUTE plan, current README runner section, and the README operator-surface contract/helper.
- Confirmed the clean boundary for this docs task is the README operator-surface test layer; planning to add a subsection helper and tighten tests in vertical slices before editing README prose.
- Next: measure current README budget, add the first failing subsection contract, then trim/reshape runner prose only as needed to satisfy the hard 1250-word cap.

- Executed slice 1 in true RED/GREEN/REFACTOR order: added a failing README subsection contract, inserted only the `### Webhook Payload Format` heading, then refactored subsection parsing into the README test helper.
- Confirmed the helper boundary needed a small correction so it returns the full subsection block including the heading; the focused test is green again and README is now 1247 words.
- Next: add the slice-2 failing contract for the row-batch example/field table/invariants, then compress runner/verify prose aggressively enough to fit the full payload docs within the 1250-word operator budget.

- Added slice 2 and slice 3 in real TDD order: failing README contract first, then compact README payload docs for row-batch, resolved, curl, and response codes.
- Word-budget risk is still the main constraint; after a broad compression pass and redundant `--log-format` cleanup the README sits at 1266 words, so only a final small trim is needed before the full test file and repo lanes.
- Next: cut ~16 words without weakening any contract, run the full README operator-surface test file, then the required `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` lanes.

- Completed the README payload-doc implementation and the boundary cleanup in `readme_operator_surface.rs`; the payload contract now lives under the README operator-surface helper/test layer instead of raw ad hoc string searches.
- Verification is complete: targeted README contract slices passed, then the full `readme_operator_surface_contract` test file passed, followed by `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all green without running `make test-long`.
- Next: mark task-07 acceptance boxes and `<passes>true</passes>`, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all task artifacts plus code/docs changes with test evidence, push, and stop.
